message:
chore: sync beads issue tracker state for Trauma Guard epic

Update issue tracker metadata for the Terminal-Level Trauma Guard epic
(ft-so7qh) sub-beads:

- ft-so7qh.1 (Core State Tracking): assign to CalmGorge agent,
  update timestamp to reflect latest implementation progress
- ft-so7qh.2 (Fuzzy Command Matching): transition from open to
  in_progress, assign to CalmGorge agent — work has begun on
  edit-distance integration with TraumaState evaluation path

No functional code changes — this is purely issue metadata bookkeeping
to reflect the current state of multi-agent work assignments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-shpr","title":"Metrics endpoint (Prometheus): wa watch --metrics bind + core counters/histograms","description":"# Task: Metrics endpoint (Prometheus)

## Goal
Expose optional Prometheus metrics to support long-running reliability:
- ingest lag
- gaps
- queue depths
- pattern detection latency
- workflow outcomes
- DB write latencies

## Design
- Disabled by default.
- Bind address configurable (default localhost).
- Must not significantly slow down hot path.

## Safety
- Default bind is localhost-only.
- Binding publicly requires an explicit, scary flag (proposed): `--dangerous-bind-any`.
- Metrics must never include transcript contents or secrets.

## Deliverables
- `wa watch --metrics <bind>` and/or config-driven enable.
- A minimal set of stable metric names.

## Testing
- Unit tests:
  - metric names are stable
  - registry can be instantiated with metrics disabled (zero overhead path)

- Integration tests:
  - start watcher with metrics enabled (bind to localhost ephemeral port)
  - fetch `/metrics` and assert:
    - response is valid Prometheus text format
    - key metrics appear (even if counts are zero)

- Safety tests:
  - default bind is localhost
  - public bind requires `--dangerous-bind-any`
  - enabling metrics does not leak secrets

## Acceptance Criteria
- When enabled, `/metrics` responds with valid Prometheus text format.
- When disabled, zero overhead on hot path.
","notes":"Closed after validation/fix pass: metrics endpoint safety contract confirmed (localhost default + explicit --dangerous-bind-any for public bind), metrics response tests passing, and quality gates restored (fmt/clippy/check).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:43:39.787589777Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T05:47:48.189398Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T22:27:35.967527253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-smm","title":"Workflow: handle_gemini_quota (Gemini API quota management)","description":"# Workflow: handle_gemini_quota

## Purpose
Handle Gemini-specific quota and rate limit scenarios:
- Daily API quota limits
- Requests-per-minute rate limits
- Model-specific token limits

## Detection Patterns
```
# Quota exhausted
"Resource has been exhausted"
"quota exceeded"
"RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED"

# Rate limit
"Too many requests"
"Please slow down"
"429 Too Many Requests"

# Token limit
"maximum token limit"
"context length exceeded"
```

## Workflow Steps

### Step 1: Detect and classify
- Parse error code/message
- Determine: quota vs rate vs token limit
- Extract reset time if available

### Step 2: Quota exhaustion handling
1. Check if alternate API key available
2. If yes: rotate to new key
3. If no: notify user with reset time
4. Persist state for resume at reset

### Step 3: Rate limit handling
1. Extract retry-after header/message
2. Implement exponential backoff
3. Resume when safe

### Step 4: Token limit handling
1. Similar to Claude Code context handling
2. Suggest conversation restart
3. Save state for continuity

## Safety Constraints
- Don't spam retries
- Respect API terms of service
- Preserve conversation state

## Configuration
```toml
[workflows.handle_gemini_quota]
api_key_rotation = false  # Require explicit enable
max_retries = 3
backoff_base_seconds = 60
```

## Testing
- Fixture: quota error patterns
- Fixture: rate limit error patterns
- Unit: backoff calculation
- E2E: quota → notification flow

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All Gemini quota types detected
- [ ] Rate limits handled with backoff
- [ ] API key rotation works when enabled
- [ ] User notified with reset times","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T19:13:38.597646095Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-30T04:41:20.879763033Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T04:41:20.879698122Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-so7qh","title":"[EPIC] Terminal-Level Trauma Guard (Deterministic Loop Interceptor)","description":"## Background
Agents frequently enter token-burning 'fix-forward death spirals', repeatedly executing trivial variations of failing commands (e.g., `cargo test`, then `cargo test -p foo`) after receiving errors. Prompt engineering cannot fix this because the LLM is too deep in its own context to realize it's looping.

## Objective
Implement a Trauma Guard that tracks recent command history and error signatures, detects trivial variations using edit distance, and blocks execution if an agent is stuck in a loop, injecting a synthetic intervention message instead.

## Justification & Philosophy
This solves the #1 cause of token waste in autonomous swarms deterministically at the multiplexer level. No magic rollbacks, no git worktrees. Just a hard stop requiring the agent to step back, read, and understand. By owning the PTY and the PolicyEngine, we can physically cut the agent off.

## Technical Approach
Leverage recently added data structures:
- `sliding_window.rs` to track recent command history per pane.
- `edit_distance.rs` to detect trivial command variations.
- `bloom_filter.rs` to quickly cache and check recent error signatures.
- `policy.rs` / `CommandGate` to enforce the block.
- PTY injection to provide the synthetic feedback.

## Success Criteria
1. The complete epic scope is delivered across child beads with no unresolved critical blockers and no loss of declared capability.
2. Every child feature/task in this epic includes comprehensive unit tests plus deterministic integration/E2E scenarios.
3. The epic includes an end-to-end validation matrix covering happy paths, failure injection, recovery behavior, and sustained/soak execution where applicable.
4. Structured logging and artifact standards are enforced across epic deliverables (timestamp, correlation_id, scenario_id, decision path, reason_code/error_code) with triage-ready evidence bundles.
5. Verification demonstrates stable, repeatable outcomes across reruns and confirms policy/safety invariants for all touched subsystems.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:12.399365Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:42:03.139452Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"]}
-{"id":"ft-so7qh.1","title":"Trauma Guard: Core State Tracking (TraumaState & SlidingWindow)","description":"## Objective
Implement the core state tracking for the Trauma Guard per-pane. 

## Details
Create a `TraumaState` struct that utilizes `sliding_window::SlidingWindow` to maintain a history of recent commands and their resulting error signatures (extracted via the PatternEngine). Use `bloom_filter::BloomFilter` for fast membership testing of recently seen error signatures to quickly flag recurring failures. This state should be accessible via the pane registry.

## Goals
- Establish the data structures for tracking.
- Ensure low memory overhead per pane.
- Provide clear APIs for recording command execution and resulting anchor matches.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Core TraumaState APIs record/read command+error-signature history deterministically under concurrent pane activity.
2. Unit tests cover happy path, edge cases (empty history, max history rollover), and error paths.
3. Property tests validate similarity-window invariants and bloom-filter false-positive bounds remain within configured tolerance.
4. Add/extend e2e scenario script that simulates repeated failed command loops and verifies deterministic block decisions and user-visible intervention output.
5. E2E artifacts must include structured logs (timestamp, pane_id, correlation_id, command_hash, decision_reason), captured stdout/stderr, and a concise summary report.
6. No regression in existing policy-gate behavior outside Trauma Guard paths.","notes":"Implemented core TraumaState + PaneRegistry integration + tests + e2e harness. Verification: rch-offloaded cargo check(workspace/all-targets)=PASS; rustup nightly fmt --check via rch=PASS; clippy -D warnings via rch fails on existing unrelated repo-wide lint debt. Remote-only e2e execution currently blocked: rch workers probe --all failing 8/8 SSH endpoints; local fallback intentionally aborted per offload-only requirement. Also patched global compile blocker in tailer.rs test attribute to unblock workspace check.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:12.642091Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-23T19:28:36.924415Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.1","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-so7qh.2","title":"Trauma Guard: Fuzzy Command Matching (Edit Distance)","description":"## Objective
Implement the similarity heuristic to detect trivial command variations.

## Details
When a new command is evaluated, compare it against recent commands in the `TraumaState` using `edit_distance::levenshtein` or `edit_distance::jaro_winkler`. 

**Critical UX Improvement (Token-Awareness):** To prevent false positives/negatives on long commands, the matcher should optionally strip common execution prefixes (e.g., `cargo`, `npm run`, `python`) before computing the edit distance, or apply a token-based Jaccard similarity. This ensures that changing a crucial flag (e.g., `cargo test --lib` vs `cargo test --all`) correctly registers as a distinct semantic intent, while trivial permutations (e.g., `cargo test -p foo` vs `cargo test -p foo -v`) are blocked.

Define configurable thresholds (e.g., >85% similarity) to classify a command as a 'trivial variation' of a previous one.

## Goals
- Connect `edit_distance` to the `TraumaState` evaluation path.
- Prevent false positives on completely different commands or crucial flag changes.
- Correctly flag trivial variations.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement full scope for this Trauma Guard capability without regressions to existing policy/command behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge/boundary behavior, and failure/error handling.
3. Add deterministic integration/end-to-end scenarios that cover loop detection, block/allow transitions, and recovery behavior.
4. Ensure tests emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, pane_id, correlation_id, scenario_id, command_hash, decision_reason/error_code) and persist failure artifacts.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:12.897604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:43:32.299882Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.2","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.2","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-so7qh.3","title":"Trauma Guard: Policy Engine Integration (CommandGate)","description":"## Objective
Wire the `TraumaState` and fuzzy matching into the `policy::CommandGate`, and provide an emergency bypass.

## Details
Before executing a command, the gate checks if the command is a trivial variation of a recently failed command (same error signature). If the threshold of consecutive failures (e.g., 3) is met, the gate must reject the command with a specific `DcgDecision::Deny` or a new `TraumaBlock` variant.

**Critical UX Improvement (Emergency Bypass):** Flaky commands exist (e.g., network curls, eventual consistency checks). If an agent or human operator *knows* they need to brute-force a command, they must be able to bypass the Trauma Guard. Implement an explicit override mechanism, such as checking for an `FT_BYPASS_TRAUMA=1` environment variable prefix on the command, which instructs the `CommandGate` to skip the loop check.

## Goals
- Intercept commands before PTY execution.
- Evaluate the Trauma Guard state.
- Deny execution if the death spiral criteria are met.
- Allow explicit bypass via `FT_BYPASS_TRAUMA=1` or similar explicit signal.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement full scope for this Trauma Guard capability without regressions to existing policy/command behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests for happy path, edge/boundary behavior, and failure/error handling.
3. Add deterministic integration/end-to-end scenarios that cover loop detection, block/allow transitions, and recovery behavior.
4. Ensure tests emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, pane_id, correlation_id, scenario_id, command_hash, decision_reason/error_code) and persist failure artifacts.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:13.156868Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:43:32.190537Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.3","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.3","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.3","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-so7qh.4","title":"Trauma Guard: Synthetic Intervention Injection (PTY Feedback)","description":"## Objective
Inject actionable feedback directly into the terminal when an agent is blocked.

## Details
When the `CommandGate` blocks an execution due to Trauma Guard, `ft` must instantly inject a synthetic, token-optimized message directly into the pane's output buffer (e.g., `[ft::TraumaGuard] 🛑 EXECUTION BLOCKED. You are in a fix-forward death spiral...`). This provides immediate, unmistakable feedback to the LLM to change its strategy (e.g., use `warp_grep` instead of guessing).

## Goals
- Format a clear, assertive message for the LLM.
- Inject the message via the PTY writer so it appears in the agent's context.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:13.431865Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:42:02.910773Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.4","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-so7qh.4","depends_on_id":"ft-so7qh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-so7qh.5","title":"Trauma Guard: Configuration and Tuning (ft.toml)","description":"## Objective
Expose Trauma Guard configuration in `ft.toml`.

## Details
Under a new `[safety.trauma_guard]` section, expose configurable parameters: `enabled` (bool), `max_consecutive_failures` (u32), `similarity_threshold` (f64), and `window_size` (usize). Implement config parsing and hot-reloading support.

## Goals
- Allow operators to tune the sensitivity.
- Support enabling/disabling without recompilation.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3.
